title: BondReplication CaseStudy
body:
Replication of a Bond Index

                                 Case Study created by Mathieu Fournier


Instructions :
    вҖў   Download the files related to this case from Moodle. Unzipped them into a folder which you can
        call HW1. When opening VS code, click file, Open Folder, and choose the directory of вҖңHW1вҖқ.
        You will then be able to open HW1_Assignment.ipynb. Remember from the installation set-up
        documents that you need to select the kernel in VS code (if not already done) to run. In the top
        right, click on вҖңSelect KernelвҖқ and then choose вҖңfins3640вҖқ.

    вҖў   Write your answer directly into the Jupyter notebook вҖңHW1_Assignment.ipynbвҖқ in the space
        provided for each one.

    вҖў   The notebook starts with a step-by-step introduction to basic data manipulations in Python. It gives
        you the required background to answer the assignment questions. Note that
        HW1_Assignment.ipynb contains all the questions, instructions and code hints, and provide space
        to answer each of the question. This word document has been created for your convenience and
        reference as you navigate HW1_Assignment.ipynb. Note that this word document is exhaustive
        and you must refer to HW1_Assignment.ipynb.

    вҖў   Constitute a team of 1 to 4 students. Clearly write down on the name and the zID of each person
        composing the team in the space provided in вҖңHW1_Assignment.ipynbвҖқ.

    вҖў   Only one group member will submit one zip file containing: (1) the Jupyter notebook
        "HW1_Assignment.ipynb" with your answers inside (2) the picture "Derivation" used for
        question 2.(a).

    вҖў   The answers of the questions must be filled in the required space directly in
        "HW1_Assignment.ipynb".

    вҖў   Please refer to the information on Moodle for the deadline.

    вҖў   Total number of points is 100 but will be adjusted to reflect the 15% contribution to course grade.



You work for an investment company specialized in bond and interest rate trading. The quantitative team
at your firm has recently studied a new bond index that reflects the performance of medium term investment
grade bonds (i.e. with 10/12 years to maturity). Based on historical data, the team of analysts has noticed
that this index, if it were investable, would constitute a great investment opportunity for your company in
order to diversify its interest rate risk. However, this index is not directly investable. Thrilled about the
promising prospects of such opportunity, your senior manager has put you in charge of finding a way to
create an investable bond portfolio that would closely replicate the index. Due to liquidity reasons, your
senior manager requires that your strategy must be based on the trading of three highly liquid bonds only.



Bond Index Replication Case                                                                      Page 1 de 5
 To help you complete the mandate, the team of quantitative analysts has collected a series of useful
information about the individual bonds in which you can invest, as well as the necessary information about
the bond index to replicate. This information is display in HW1_Assignment.ipynb. This Jupyter notebook
contains various useful information and guide you through the assignment. It also loads two excel files with
information regarding the three tradeable bonds including the bondвҖҷs yield-to-maturity in the first file, and
the bond index daily value, modified duration, and its convexity in the other.

Important note: To simplify the replication exercise, we assume that the individual bonds have fixed
maturities at all time. For instance, Bond 1 is priced every day using a fixed maturity of 11 years regardless
of the trading day. The same is true for Bond 2 and 3. As a result, you will disregard the passage of time in
all calculations. That is, you should assume a fixed maturity for each bond regardless of the trading day
when calculating the price, duration, modified duration, and convexity of each of the three bonds (i.e. T is
fixed in all formulas).

Question 1: (40 points)
You first compute some important variables that will help you conduct the analysis.

    a) Using the information provided in HW1_Assignment.ipynb, calculate the price of each bond for
       each of the 2920 trading days that constitute your sample period. You will use the bond pricing
       formula given in class. See HW1_Assignment.ipynb for the details and for answering the question.
       (15 points)

    b) Using the information provided in HW1_Assignment.ipynb, calculate the duration and modified
       duration of each bond for each of the 2920 trading days that constitute your sample period. You
       will use the duration formula for coupon bonds given in class. See HW1_Assignment.ipynb for
       the details and for answering the question. (15 points)

    c) Using the information provided in HW1_Assignment.ipynb, calculate the convexity of each bond
       for each of the 2920 trading days that constitute your sample period. See HW1_Assignment.ipynb
       for the details and for answering the question. (10 points).


Question 2: (35 points)
You now have all the required information to implement a replication strategy for the bond index. You
decide to start with the simplest replication strategy. Your task is to create a bond portfolio that matches
the bond indexвҖҷs value and modified duration every day. For this question, you are restricted to trade only
Bonds 1 and 3. Moreover, your replication will be based on a daily rebalancing frequency of your portfolio.
More precisely, you will determine the optimal quantities to invest in each bond at the closing of each day
t, and you will hold the portfolio constructed for one day (i.e. you unwind your position at the closing of
the next day t+1).

To make sure that you do the computation correctly, you decide to read an article on bond portfolio
replication. The article provides you with an algorithm that you can follow. Let us denote by рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј and рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј the
value of the bond index and its modified duration, respectively, at the closing of day t. Moreover, denote
by рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 the quantity to invest in Bonds 1 and 3, while рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 and рқҗөрқ‘Ў3 denote their price, and рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 and рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3
correspond to their modified duration. Based on this notation, the value of the replication portfolio is


Bond Index Replication Case                                                                       Page 2 de 5
                                           рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ = рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3 ,

and its modified duration is given by

                                                рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў1Г—рқҗөрқ‘Ў1             рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3Г—рқҗөрқ‘Ў3
                                      рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ =     рқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ     Г— рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 +     рқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ     Г— рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3 ,
                                                рқ‘үрқ‘Ў                  рқ‘үрқ‘Ў


At the closing of day t, your goal is to find рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 such that the replication portfolio has the same value
as the index and has the same modified duration, such that

                                                    рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ
                                                    рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ

This can be written as function of the рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3

                                 рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3 (рқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘ўрқ‘Һрқ‘Ўрқ‘–рқ‘ңрқ‘ӣ #1)
                                 рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1     1+ рқ‘Ў
                                                   рқ‘ӣ3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3
                           рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј =           Г— рқҗ·рқ‘Ў             Г— рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3 (рқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘ўрқ‘Һрқ‘Ўрқ‘–рқ‘ңрқ‘ӣ #2)
                                     рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј               рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј

where we have used the fact that рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ to define the denominator in equation (#2). Because we have
two equations and two unknowns (i.e. рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 ), we can solve for рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 as functions of рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј , рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј , рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 ,
рқҗөрқ‘Ў3 , рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 , and рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3 . We denote рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—      3вҲ—
                                  рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў the optimal quantities that solve the two equations above. These
                        1вҲ—      3вҲ—
optimal quantities рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў satisfy:

                                          рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј (рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј вҲ’ рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3 )
                                    рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—
                                     рқ‘Ў =                   (рқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘ўрқ‘Һрқ‘Ўрқ‘–рқ‘ңрқ‘ӣ #3)
                                          рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 (рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 вҲ’ рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3 )
                                          рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј (рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј вҲ’ рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 )
                                    рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3вҲ— = 3 3             (рқҗёрқ‘һрқ‘ўрқ‘Һрқ‘Ўрқ‘–рқ‘ңрқ‘ӣ #4)
                                          рқҗөрқ‘Ў (рқҗ·рқ‘Ў вҲ’ рқҗ·рқ‘Ў1 )


Given рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—     3вҲ—
       рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў , the value of the replication portfolio at the closing of the next day is now given by

                                        рқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ
                                      рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1  = рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—   1      3вҲ—   3
                                               рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 ,

and the % relative replication error is
                                                            рқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ    рқҗј )
                                                          (рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1 вҲ’рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1
                                          рқҗёрқ‘ҹрқ‘ҹрқ‘ңрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ў+1 =           рқҗј
                                                             рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1
                                                                         Г— 100.

Finally, on day t+1 the new quantities рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—       3вҲ—                                     рқҗј      рқҗј     1     3
                                        рқ‘Ў+1 and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў+1 need to be calculated based on рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1 , рқҗ·рқ‘Ў+1 , рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 , рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 ,
  1          3                         1вҲ—       3вҲ—
рқҗ·рқ‘Ў+1 , and рқҗ·рқ‘Ў+1 . Based on the values рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў+1 and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў+1 , as well as on the realization of prices, the new relative
replicating error рқҗёрқ‘ҹрқ‘ҹрқ‘ңрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ў+2 can now be calculated. You can repeat this algorithm every trading day until the
end of the replication horizon.

    a) Using the above notations, show how to obtain the expressions for рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—        3вҲ—
                                                                             рқ‘Ў and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў in equations (#3)
       and (#4) from equations (#1) and (#2). For this question, you can write down your derivation on a
       separate piece of paper, take a picture of your derivation, and save it in the HW1 folder. See


Bond Index Replication Case                                                                           Page 3 de 5
         HW1_Assignment.ipynb for the instructions. Note: Make sure that your derivation is readable
        when pasting it. (15 points)

    b) Using equations (#3) and (#4), compute the daily quantities to invest in Bonds 1 and 3, as well as
       the value of the replication portfolio and the relative replication error. Do not round the quantities
       obtained to the closest integer (i.e. we are assuming for simplicity that we can trade fraction of
       bonds). See HW1_Assignment.ipynb for the details. (15 points)

    c) Briefly interpret your results and the graph of the relative errors obtained. What do you conclude
       from the graph? Can you suggest a potential way to improve the replication? Answer in 5 lines
       maximum in the space provided in HW1_Assignment.ipynb. (5 points)




Question 3: (25 points)
You now decide to investigate the performance of a replication strategy that would account for the bond
indexвҖҷs convexity. In order to match the convexity and the modified duration of the index, an additional
bond must be traded. For this exercise, your replication strategy will thus involve Bonds 1, 2, and 3. All the
notations remain unchanged. However, we now introduce рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқҗј , which denotes the bond index convexity, and
рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў1 , рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў2 , and рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў3 , which are the individual bond convexities.

At the closing of day t, your goal is to find the quantities рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў , рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў2 and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 such that the replication portfolio
has the same value as the index and has the same modified duration and convexity:

                                                   рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ
                                                   рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ
                                                   рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ

The problem now implies three equations and three unknowns:

                                      рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў2 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў2 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3
                                  рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1      1+ рқ‘Ў
                                                     рқ‘ӣ2 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў2      2+ рқ‘Ў
                                                                       рқ‘ӣ3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3
                            рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқҗј =           Г—  рқҗ· рқ‘Ў            Г—  рқҗ· рқ‘Ў            Г— рқҗ·рқ‘Ў3
                                      рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј               рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј               рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј
                                  рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў1      1+ рқ‘Ў
                                                     рқ‘ӣ2 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў2      2+ рқ‘Ў
                                                                       рқ‘ӣ3 Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў3
                            рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқҗј =           Г—  рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў             Г—  рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў             Г— рқҗ¶рқ‘Ў3 ,
                                      рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј               рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј               рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј

where the formulas on the right of the equal sign correspond to рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ , рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ , and рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ , respectively, and
where we have used the fact that рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқҗј = рқ‘үрқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ to define the denominator in рқҗ·рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ and рқҗ¶рқ‘Ўрқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ . From these
equations, we can solve for рқ‘ӣ1рқ‘Ў , рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў2 , and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў3 . Given the optimal рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—   2вҲ—       3вҲ—
                                                                     рқ‘Ў , рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў , and рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў , the value of the replication
portfolio at the closing of the next day is given by

                                рқ‘…рқ‘’рқ‘қ
                              рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1  = рқ‘ӣ1вҲ—   1      2вҲ—   2      3вҲ—   3
                                       рқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 + рқ‘ӣрқ‘Ў Г— рқҗөрқ‘Ў+1 ,

and the % relative replication error is:

                                                  (рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1 вҲ’рқ‘үрқ‘Ў+1
                                     рқҗёрқ‘ҹрқ‘ҹрқ‘ңрқ‘ҹрқ‘Ў+1 =         рқҗј        Г— 100.
